steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize how the French Republican calendar honored rural life. The relevant note is: "Each day was named after a plant, animal, agricultural tool, or mineral as a way of honoring an aspect of rural life." The answer must state that purpose and support it with a concrete example from the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Each day in the French Republican calendar was given a name honoring an aspect of rural life; for example, a day in Brumaire was named for an animal, the goose.': states the purpose (honoring rural life) and backs it with a specific example (Oie = goose, an animal). ✓
  • Choice B — 'The second month in the French Republican calendar, Brumaire, had three weeks that were divided into ten days each.': describes the calendar's structure, not how it honored rural life. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Each day in the French Republican calendar was given a unique name; the fifth day of Brumaire was no exception.': emphasizes that names are unique, not that they honored rural life. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Each day in the French Republican calendar's month of Brumaire had a unique name.': same problem as C — focuses on uniqueness, not the rural-life purpose. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
